commands.meaning: Shows Linux network interfaces, link state, MAC addresses, and assigned IP addresses.

diagnosis_rules.diagnosis: The Linux interface reports no carrier.
diagnosis_rules.meaning: The NIC does not detect physical link.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not change IP routes or DNS until physical link is restored.

diagnosis_rules.diagnosis: A Linux network interface appears down.
diagnosis_rules.meaning: The interface is not currently operational for normal traffic.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not bring interfaces up on production systems without confirming network role and approval.

diagnosis_rules.diagnosis: The Linux endpoint appears to have a link-local IPv4 address.
diagnosis_rules.meaning: A 169.254.x.x address usually indicates DHCP did not provide a usable IPv4 lease.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not assign a static IP until VLAN, DHCP, and gateway details are confirmed.

next_commands.why: Checks the default gateway and routing table.
next_commands.meaning: Shows kernel routes and default path.
Sends ICMP echo requests to the gateway.

next_commands.why: Checks whether the endpoint has learned local neighbor MAC addresses.

commands.learning_note: On Linux, link state and IP state are separate; an interface can have configuration but still lack carrier.
commands.source_note: Local command knowledge base. Verify exact command behavior against distro and tool version differences.

commands.meaning: Shows the Linux routing table, including default gateway and connected routes.

diagnosis_rules.diagnosis: The Linux route table contains an unreachable or prohibited route.
diagnosis_rules.meaning: Traffic matching that route may be intentionally blocked by routing policy.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not remove or change routes on production systems without approval and rollback.

diagnosis_rules.diagnosis: A route references an interface that is down.
diagnosis_rules.meaning: Traffic for that route may fail until the interface link is restored.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not add alternate routes until interface state and design are confirmed.

diagnosis_rules.diagnosis: The pasted route table contains a default route.
diagnosis_rules.meaning: The system has an outbound path for non-local destinations.
possible_causes: No route issue is visible from the default route alone
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not change the default route without confirming active sessions and rollback.

next_commands.why: Checks whether the route interface has a valid address and link state.
next_commands.meaning: Shows interface state and assigned IP addresses.

next_commands.why: Checks whether the default gateway is reachable.
next_commands.meaning: Sends ICMP echo requests to the gateway IP.

commands.learning_note: A route table shows intended forwarding; ping and ARP show whether the next hop responds.
commands.source_note: Local command knowledge base. Verify exact command behavior against distro and tool version differences.

commands.meaning: Tests reachability to an IP address or hostname using ICMP echo.
use_for: Compare DNS name and IP tests

diagnosis_rules.diagnosis: The ping test did not receive replies.
diagnosis_rules.meaning: The target may be offline, ICMP may be blocked, or routing may be broken.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not assume the application is down based only on ICMP.

diagnosis_rules.diagnosis: The local system or gateway reports the destination is unreachable.
diagnosis_rules.meaning: The endpoint may lack a valid route or Layer 2 path.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not change routes until IP, link, and gateway details are confirmed.

diagnosis_rules.diagnosis: The hostname could not be resolved.
diagnosis_rules.meaning: DNS failed before ping could test network reachability to the target.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not change resolver configuration until direct IP connectivity and DNS server reachability are checked.

next_commands.why: Checks local interface state and IP assignment.
next_commands.meaning: Shows interfaces and assigned IP addresses.

next_commands.why: Checks default gateway and route selection.
Checks DNS resolution when pinging a name fails.

commands.learning_note: Ping should be interpreted with IP configuration, routing, DNS, and firewall behavior in mind.
commands.source_note: Local command knowledge base. Verify exact command behavior against distro and tool version differences.

commands.meaning: Shows hop-by-hop path toward a target.
use_for: Compare local gateway and upstream reachability

diagnosis_rules.meaning: Some devices do not respond to traceroute; repeated early timeouts are more important.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not assume one timeout hop is the fault if later hops continue.

diagnosis_rules.diagnosis: Traceroute could not resolve the target name.
diagnosis_rules.meaning: DNS failed before route testing began.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not change routes before DNS resolution is checked.

next_commands.why: Checks route selection and default gateway.

next_commands.meaning: Sends ICMP echo requests to the gateway.

commands.learning_note: Traceroute output is path evidence, but filtering can hide healthy hops.
commands.source_note: Local command knowledge base. Verify exact command behavior against distro and tool version differences.

good_output: Gateway IP has a MAC address

diagnosis_rules.meaning: The endpoint has not resolved the IP address to a MAC address.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not change routes until link, IP, and gateway reachability are confirmed.

next_commands.why: Generates traffic to the gateway and tests reachability.
next_commands.meaning: Sends ICMP echo requests to the gateway.

next_commands.why: Checks link and IP state on the local interface.
next_commands.meaning: Shows network interface state and addresses.

commands.learning_note: ARP is local-subnet evidence; it does not prove remote internet or application reachability.
commands.source_note: Local command knowledge base. Verify exact command behavior against distro and tool version differences.

commands.meaning: Queries DNS records and displays resolver response details.

diagnosis_rules.diagnosis: DNS queries are timing out or no DNS server is reachable.
diagnosis_rules.meaning: The endpoint may not be able to reach its configured resolver.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not change resolver configuration until IP connectivity to DNS servers is checked.

diagnosis_rules.diagnosis: DNS responded that the name does not exist.
diagnosis_rules.meaning: The queried hostname may be incorrect or missing from DNS.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not change DNS servers until the hostname and expected zone are confirmed.

diagnosis_rules.meaning: The resolver or upstream authoritative path could not answer successfully.
diagnosis_rules.do_not_touch: Do not change endpoint network settings before comparing another resolver or known-good client.

next_commands.why: Checks whether the endpoint has a default route to reach DNS servers.
Compares direct IP reachability with DNS behavior.
next_commands.meaning: Tests reachability to an IP address.

commands.learning_note: DNS evidence is strongest when you compare the resolver, the returned status, and direct IP reachability.
commands.source_note: Local command knowledge base. Verify exact command behavior against distro and tool version differences.
